name: Sendero de la Hoz de Alarcón – running circular from Alarcón
name_original: Sendero de la Hoz de Alarcón – running circular desde Alarcón
region_intro: Jogging around Alarcón offers diverse terrain shaped by the Júcar River and its dramatic gorges. The region features a mix of landscapes, from riverbanks and the Alarcón Reservoir to hills and pine forests. This varied environment provides both physical challenges and scenic routes for runners. Best jogging routes around Alarcón The most popular jogging route is Sendero de la Hoz de Alarcón – running circular from Alarcón, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 30 minutes to complete. This route navigates the dramatic Júcar River gorges, offering challenging sections and scenic views. Another top favourite among local runners is Embalse de Alarcón – running circular from Olmedilla de Alarcón, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This route follows the shores of the Alarcón Reservoir, providing flatter sections and expansive water views. Local runners also love the Casa de la Pradera loop from El Picazo, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 52 minutes. Jogging around Alarcón is defined by the Júcar River gorges, the Alarcón Reservoir, and surrounding pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult trails. The routes in Alarcón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Alarcón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around Alarcón?
A: The Alarcón region offers a diverse range of terrain for runners. You'll find dramatic sections along the &lt;strong&gt;Júcar River gorges&lt;/strong&gt;, flatter paths around the &lt;strong&gt;Alarcón Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt;, and varied landscapes including hills and pine forests. This mix provides options for different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails in Alarcón for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, the region features routes with significant elevation changes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014657455/sendero-de-la-hoz-de-alarcon-running-circular-from-alarcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de la Hoz de Alarcón – running circular from Alarcón&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.1 miles (11.4 km) long and navigates the dramatic Júcar River gorges, offering a more difficult experience.

Q: Can I find easier or moderate jogging paths in the Alarcón area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Alarcón are suitable for moderate fitness levels. For a scenic, flatter run, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014675865/embalse-de-alarcon-running-circular-from-olmedilla-de-alarcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón – running circular from Olmedilla de Alarcón&lt;/a&gt;. This 5.7-mile (9.2 km) path follows the shores of the Alarcón Reservoir, providing expansive water views and a less strenuous experience.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in Alarcón?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Alarcón are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014657455/sendero-de-la-hoz-de-alarcon-running-circular-from-alarcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de la Hoz de Alarcón&lt;/a&gt; and the more moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014675865/embalse-de-alarcon-running-circular-from-olmedilla-de-alarcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón&lt;/a&gt; routes.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see while jogging in Alarcón?
A: Alarcón's routes are rich in history and natural beauty. You can jog with constant views of the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón Castle&lt;/a&gt;, explore paths along the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River gorges&lt;/a&gt;, or cross the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5611845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Henchidero Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The town's medieval walled enclosure also provides a unique backdrop for your run.

Q: How many jogging routes are available in the Alarcón area?
A: There are over 5 dedicated jogging routes around Alarcón, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: What do other runners say about jogging in Alarcón?
A: The running routes in Alarcón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from users. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic views of the Júcar River and the historic castle.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass through parks or green spaces near Alarcón?
A: Yes, you can find routes that take you through pleasant green areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24528728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hondo del Río Park loop from Villalgordo del Júcar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.9-mile (11.0 km) route that offers a refreshing run through a park setting.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Alarcón Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014675865/embalse-de-alarcon-running-circular-from-olmedilla-de-alarcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón – running circular from Olmedilla de Alarcón&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase the reservoir. This route provides beautiful views of the water and its natural surroundings, extending approximately 9 km.

Q: Can I find a moderate running route that is not too long?
A: For a moderate run that's not excessively long, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de la Pradera loop from El Picazo&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 4.9 miles (7.8 km) and typically completed in about 52 minutes, offering varied terrain.

Q: Are there any running routes that explore the areas surrounding Alarcón village?
A: Many routes allow you to explore the wider area. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casasimarro loop from Casasimarro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.4-mile (10.3 km) route that takes you through the countryside surrounding a nearby settlement.
tips: The trail through the Hoces de Alarcón is a spectacular route that allows you to enjoy impressive landscapes and the rich history of the area.
